title: Filtering Noise in Observations
concept: Removing stack traces, boilerplate UI elements, and repeat records to save token space.

exercises.prompt: Last lesson: why turn a messy terminal stream or raw HTML into structured data first?

options.text: So the AI can reason over clean, organized info instead of a tangled mess
Because raw text is illegal to give an AI
To make the observation longer and harder to read

exercises.explain: Structured observations organize raw streams into clear fields, so the model spends its attention on meaning instead of untangling formatting.

cards.title: Drowning in Junk
cards.body: When an agent looks at a webpage or error log, most of what it sees is repeated menus, styling, and giant error dumps. All that clutter costs tokens and distracts the model.

cards.title: Filtering Noise
cards.body: Noise filtering means deleting the useless parts of an observation before the AI reads it. We cut stack traces, boilerplate UI, and duplicate records to keep only what matters.

cards.title: Like a Highlighter
cards.body: Imagine reading a 20-page report but only highlighting the 3 key lines. The agent works better when you hand it the highlights, not the whole stack of paper.

cards.title: Trimming a Stack Trace
cards.body: An error dump has 200 lines of internal file paths. Filtering keeps just the top line: 'FileNotFoundError: config.json missing.' That one line is all the agent needs to act.

cards.title: Myth: More Info Is Always Better
cards.body: Not true! Extra noise wastes tokens and hides the important clue. A shorter, cleaner observation often helps the agent decide faster and more accurately.

cards.title: Noisy vs Filtered
cards.body: Same webpage, two versions the agent could receive.

left.text: Full HTML: nav bars, ads, styling, 50 repeated list items

right.text: Just the 5 unique search results the task actually needs

exercises.prompt: Which of these is 'noise' an agent should usually filter out of an observation?

options.text: A 150-line stack trace repeating internal file paths
The single error message that names what broke
The one search result matching the task
The current price the user asked about

exercises.explain: The long stack trace is mostly internal clutter. Keeping the key error line and cutting the rest saves tokens without losing the useful signal.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Removing duplicate records from an observation helps save token space.
exercises.explain: Repeated records add length without adding new information. Deduplicating keeps the observation compact so more room is left for what matters.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about cleaning observations.
exercises.sentence: Cutting boilerplate UI elements like menus and ads reduces noise in an observation.
exercises.explain: Boilerplate UI elements are repeated clutter, so removing them cuts the noise and leaves the useful signal.

exercises.prompt: Which observation summary was AI-generated to be clean and filtered?

options.text: Result: 3 items found. Cheapest: Blue Mug $8. In stock.
ERROR at line 44 ... at line 45 ... at line 46 ... [full stack dump]

exercises.explain: The AI-filtered version keeps only the task-relevant facts in a compact form, while the others are raw noise the agent would waste tokens reading.

exercises.prompt: An agent scrapes a store page. The raw observation has: nav menus, 30 identical 'Add to cart' buttons, a 100-line JS error trace, and the 4 product names/prices. To save tokens AND keep the task useful, what should you send the model?

options.text: Just the 4 product names and prices, plus the one-line error summary if relevant
The entire raw page so the model 'has everything'
Only the nav menus and buttons since they appear most often
The full 100-line stack trace and nothing else

exercises.explain: Good filtering keeps the signal (product names, prices, one key error line) and drops the noise (menus, repeated buttons, long traces). This saves token space while preserving everything the agent needs to act. Remember: cut stack traces, boilerplate UI, and duplicate records, but never the facts the task depends on.
